Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists AK111691 for OsMPK6.

Applied_Species.reason: Experiments were performed in rice cultured cells and rice seedlings.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The paper is on rice; Oryza sativa is directly inferable from the title and context of rice molecular studies.

Source_Species.reason: OsMPK6 is a rice gene studied in rice cells.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Os genes in this rice paper are from Oryza sativa.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: OsMPK6 is explicitly described as a MAPK/mitogen-activated protein kinase.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: Results directly measured momilactones and phytocassanes and linked their accumulation to OsMPK6.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: The paper explicitly refers to momilactones and phytocassanes as diterpenoid phytoalexins.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The summary and discussion describe these metabolites as anti-/antimicrobial defense compounds.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Figure 1b,c measured these phytoalexins in rice cell culture media.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results show osmpk6 has reduced elicitor-induced momilactone/phytocassane accumulation, complementation restores it, and OsMPK6 is essential for OsMKK4DD-induced cell death.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: OsMPK6 is implicated in OsMKK4DD-induced cell death and the cascade is associated with repression of basic cellular activities, but the paper does not explicitly present these as a direct tradeoff specific to OsMPK6 alone.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: OsMKK4 and OsMKK5 are shown to activate OsMPK6 in vitro/in vivo contexts, but direct physical interaction is not demonstrated; OsMPK3 is part of the same cascade but not shown to interact directly with OsMPK6.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in the Results/Methods: osmpk6 mutant and complementation, in-gel kinase assay, IP kinase assay, chitin elicitor, HPLC-ESI-MS/MS, and microarray comparison in osmpk6 vs osmpk6/OsMPK6.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Chitin elicitor and DEX-inducible OsMKK4DD are central treatments used for OsMPK6-related assays.

Omics_Data.reason: Microarray data are reported and GEO accessions GSE15613 and GSE18787 are given in Methods.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point is directly supported by Results: activation by elicitor, reduced phytoalexins in osmpk6 with rescue by complementation, OsMPK6 dependence of diterpenoid pathway gene induction, and essentiality for OsMKK4DD-induced cell death.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper concludes OsMPK6 plays the major role in diterpenoid phytoalexin synthesis, while OsMPK3 may act redundantly for other responses.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: OsMPK6 kinase activity is described as immediately and transiently activated after chitin elicitor treatment.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: This matches the cultured rice cell system used in the Results.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: This is a forward-looking application claim not directly tested in the paper, though it is a plausible implication.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The osmpk6/OsMPK6 line is described as harboring OsMPK6 cDNA fused with its upstream promoter sequence, and OsMKK4DD was expressed from a DEX-inducible promoter.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists AK120525 for OsMKK4.

Applied_Species.reason: The experiments on OsMKK4 were carried out in rice cultured cells and seedlings.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice in this paper refers to Oryza sativa.

Source_Species.reason: OsMKK4 is a rice gene studied in rice.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: OsMKK4 is from Oryza sativa in this rice study.

Regulator_Type.reason: OsMKK4 is explicitly identified as a MAPK kinase/MAPKK.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: The Results state OsMKK4 belongs to group C MAPKKs.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: OsMKK4DD induced accumulation of momilactones, phytocassanes, and lignin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These are explicitly diterpenoid phytoalexins and lignin is a phenylpropanoid-derived polymer; the exact phrase phenylpropanoid polymer is inferable.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The paper frames diterpenoid phytoalexins as antimicrobial compounds and lignin as a defense-associated product.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Momilactones/phytocassanes were measured from culture media, and lignin was quantified as thioglycolic-acid extractable cell-wall complexes.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results support OsMKK4 as a positive regulator of metabolic flow toward diterpenoid phytoalexins and lignin under elicitor/OsMKK4DD conditions.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: OsMKK4 activates OsMPK3/OsMPK6 and induces DXP, diterpenoid pathway genes including CPS2, CPS4, KSL4, KSL7, KSL8, plus phenylpropanoid genes.

Omics_Data.reason: Microarray analysis with GEO accessions GSE15613 and GSE18787 is explicitly reported.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Chitin elicitor activation of OsMKK4 and DEX-inducible OsMKK4DD expression are directly shown.

Other_Important_Info.reason: All listed statements are supported: no extracellular ROS in rice, ROS/cell death in N. benthamiana, and differential OsMPK6 dependence of diterpenoid vs phenylpropanoid induction.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: OsMPK3 and OsMPK6 are direct downstream kinases shown; DXP/diterpenoid/phenylpropanoid genes listed are induced by OsMKK4DD in Results.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: The Results and Summary explicitly describe defense-associated metabolic reprogramming toward diterpenoid phytoalexins and lignin/phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: OsMKK4DD induced cell death, seedling death/growth arrest, and transcriptomic repression of translation/cell-cycle genes.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The paper concludes the OsMKK4-OsMPK6 cascade plays a crucial role in reprogramming metabolism to diterpenoid phytoalexin biosynthesis after fungal MAMP signaling.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered point matches reported Results.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: The paper supports involvement in a MAPK cascade with OsMPK3/OsMPK6, but does not demonstrate a stable protein complex.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are directly used in the paper.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: IP kinase assay showed OsMKK4 activity increased within 5 min after chitin elicitor treatment.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: OsMPK3/OsMPK6 are kinase substrates/targets and OsMPK4 weakly activated, but direct physical interactions are not shown.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Chitin elicitor, a fungal MAMP, activates OsMKK4.

Regulation_Mode.reason: OsMKK4 acts by kinase-mediated activation of downstream MAPKs and consequent transcriptional reprogramming.

Applied_Promoters.reason: OsMKK4WT and OsMKK4DD were expressed under a DEX-inducible promoter.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Accession numbers section lists AK067339 for OsMPK3.

Applied_Species.reason: OsMPK3 was studied in rice cultured cells.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: OsMPK3 is from Oryza sativa in this study.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: It is described as a mitogen-activated protein kinase.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The paper does not directly demonstrate OsMPK3 regulates accumulation of momilactones, phytocassanes, or lignin; only possible redundancy is discussed.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: These metabolite classes exist in the paper, but their linkage to OsMPK3 specifically is not established.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Defense functions of the metabolites are supported, but not specifically as OsMPK3-controlled outputs.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Those accumulation sites are correct for the metabolites, but OsMPK3 was not directly tied to their accumulation measurements.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: Results directly show OsMPK3 is activated by chitin elicitor and by OsMKK4DD; the statement carefully notes its essential role was not directly demonstrated.

Omics_Data.reason: Microarray datasets are in the paper, though not OsMPK3-specific; still the data are part of the study context.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Chitin elicitor and DEX-inducible OsMKK4DD were used in OsMPK3 activation assays.

Other_Important_Info.reason: This accurately reflects that OsMPK3 is experimentally studied but its causal metabolic role is weaker than OsMPK6 and unresolved genetically.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: OsMPK3 is part of defense MAPK signaling, but control of metabolic reprogramming by OsMPK3 specifically is not demonstrated.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The paper only notes inability to obtain OsMPK3 knockdown in osmpk6 OsMKK4DD cells and suggests lethality; this is suggestive, not definitive.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: The field explicitly says decisive influence was not established and only redundancy is suggested, which matches the paper.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three points match the Results/Discussion, including the caveat that redundancy was not directly proven.

Protein_Complex_Involvement.reason: Participation in a MAPK cascade with upstream OsMKK4 is supported, but a protein complex is not shown.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: IP kinase assay, in vitro kinase assay with OsMKK4, and DEX-inducible OsMKK4DD activation assays were used.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: OsMPK3 was activated by chitin elicitor and after OsMKK4DD induction in cultured rice cells.

Interacting_Proteins.reason: OsMKK4 and OsMKK5 activate OsMPK3 in vitro/in vivo contexts, but direct physical interaction with them or OsMPK6 is not demonstrated.

Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs.reason: Chitin elicitor activates OsMPK3, and OsMKK4 is shown to be an upstream kinase.

Regulation_Mode.reason: OsMPK3 functions in protein kinase signaling.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The DEX-inducible promoter was used for upstream OsMKK4DD expression in assays that activated OsMPK3.
